page: Vultr voc-s-24c-192gb-3840s-amd is an AMD EPYC-Rome based server belonging to the Optimized Cloud Compute family. It features 24 vCPUs and 24 physical cores with an x86_64 architecture, operating at a base clock speed of 2.0 GHz. The system is configured with a shared CPU allocation. It includes 192.0 GB of system memory, yielding 8.0 GB of RAM per core, and is bundled with 3840 GB of local NVMe SSD storage.

Benchmark data indicates strong multi-core and memory performance. The server achieved a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 67368.14 and a PassMark CPU score of 51919.27, both placing in the top 25% of tested systems. Memory bandwidth is particularly strong, with large-block read-only speeds reaching 458.63 GB/sec and write speeds at 223.24 GB/sec, placing the system in the top 10% for memory throughput. Conversely, single-threaded operations, such as gzip compression at 29.22 GB/sec and single-core stress-ng at 2839.58, perform in the middle 50% tier, reflecting the moderate 2.0 GHz clock speed of the EPYC-Rome processor.

The combination of a shared CPU allocation with a high memory-to-core ratio of 1:8 and substantial local NVMe storage offers a cost-efficient profile for memory-intensive workloads that do not require sustained, dedicated single-core compute. However, the shared CPU allocation represents a tradeoff for workloads requiring guaranteed, non-burstable CPU performance. This server is configured for memory-optimized tasks, database operations, large-scale caching, and moderate-scale multi-threaded data processing.
description: Vultr voc-s-24c-192gb-3840s-amd is an AMD EPYC-Rome server in the Optimized Cloud Compute family, featuring 24 vCPUs, 192.0 GB of RAM, and 3840 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. Operating on an x86_64 architecture at 2.0 GHz with a shared CPU allocation, it delivers a 1:8 memory-to-vCPU ratio. In benchmarks, the server demonstrates top 10% memory bandwidth, reaching 458.63 GB/sec for large-block reads, and top 25% multi-core CPU performance with a PassMark score of 51919.27. Single-threaded performance and database operations align with average tier results. The shared CPU allocation presents a tradeoff for sustained compute, but the high memory density and local NVMe storage make this configuration highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads, database hosting, and multi-threaded data processing.
og_description: Vultr voc-s-24c-192gb-3840s-amd is an AMD EPYC-Rome server offering 24 vCPUs, 192 GB RAM, and 3840 GB NVMe storage, delivering top-tier memory bandwidth for memory-intensive and database workloads.

tagline: An AMD-powered server combining high memory density and local NVMe storage for memory-intensive and database workloads.

192.0 GB of system memory providing a high 1:8 vCPU-to-RAM ratio
3840 GB of integrated local NVMe SSD storage
Top 10% memory bandwidth performance with 458.63 GB/sec large-block reads
Shared CPU allocation offering a cost-efficient profile for memory-heavy tasks
Suited for database hosting, caching layers, and multi-threaded data processing
